description:
You are a build-engineer who has to move freshly produced artifacts into a central
    repository and write a release log that the QA robots can later audit.

    1.  A text file listing the artifacts that **must** be present for
        today’s drop is already on disk:

            /home/user/expected_release.txt

        It contains one artifact name per line **without** any file-name
        extension, for example:

            alpha-0.1
            beta-2.3

    2.  Compressed artifact bundles arrive in:

            /home/user/incoming/

        Each bundle is named “<artifact>.tar.gz”.
        Two of the required bundles have already arrived;
        one has deliberately been left out so that you can
        demonstrate error handling.

    3.  The place where finished bundles must be stored is:

            /home/user/repository/

        •  If that directory does not exist, create it.  
        •  Move (do **not** copy) every *existing* bundle that is mentioned in
           expected_release.txt from /incoming/ to /repository/.

    4.  After processing every line in expected_release.txt
        write an **audit log** file to:

            /home/user/release.log

        The log must contain exactly one line **per line** of
        expected_release.txt, preserving the original order.
        Use the following strict format (the test harness
        will parse it literally):

            OK    <artifact> <YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ>
            ERROR <artifact> <YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ> NOT_FOUND

        Details:
        •  “OK” if the bundle was successfully moved,  
           “ERROR” if the corresponding .tar.gz file did **not** exist.  
        •  The timestamp must be in UTC and use the literal
           capital “Z” suffix (ISO-8601).  
        •  Separate the three (or four) fields with a single ASCII
           space; no tabs, no extra spaces.  
        •  No header or footer lines—only the status lines.

    5.  Nothing should be printed to stdout/stderr except what your
        commands naturally emit.  The automated checker will only look
        at:
            •  the presence of /home/user/repository/,
            •  which .tar.gz files ended up inside it,
            •  and the exact contents of /home/user/release.log.

    The whole job can be done with three or four well-chosen shell
    commands.  Make sure your log file conforms **exactly** to the format
    above; otherwise the QA robots will fail your build.
truth:
Pre-existing state before the agent starts:

    Directories:
        /home/user/
        /home/user/incoming/

    Files and their contents:
        1. /home/user/expected_release.txt
           (3 lines, LF line endings, final newline present)
                core-1.0
                util-1.2
                gui-3.5

        2. /home/user/incoming/core-1.0.tar.gz
           – tarball containing a single file:
                core.txt
                (plain text, contents: "This is the core artifact\n")

        3. /home/user/incoming/util-1.2.tar.gz
           – tarball containing a single file:
                util.txt
                (plain text, contents: "This is the util artifact\n")

        4. /home/user/incoming/   (no gui-3.5.tar.gz on purpose)

    Files/directories that **must** exist when the agent finishes:

        A. /home/user/repository/           (directory)
               /home/user/repository/core-1.0.tar.gz
               /home/user/repository/util-1.2.tar.gz
           (The tarballs must have been moved, **not** copied; therefore
            they must no longer be present in /home/user/incoming/.)

        B. /home/user/incoming/
               (must no longer contain core-1.0.tar.gz nor util-1.2.tar.gz;
                absence of gui-3.5.tar.gz is fine.)

        C. /home/user/release.log
           •  Exactly 3 lines, in this order:
                1) "OK core-1.0 <ISO8601-UTC>"
                2) "OK util-1.2 <ISO8601-UTC>"
                3) "ERROR gui-3.5 <ISO8601-UTC> NOT_FOUND"
           •  The checker will validate:
                – the leading keywords (“OK”/“ERROR”),
                – the artifact name,
                – that the timestamp matches the
                  regex `[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z`,
                – and that “NOT_FOUND” appears only on the third line.
